nwtc_callees.note: Called by internal Failed() wrapper and by RotCalcOutput, FVW_CalcOutput, RotCavtCrit sub-callees
Called via FVW_CalcOutput and RotCavtCrit sub-callees
Called via FVW_CalcOutput and RotCavtCrit for error message formatting
Called via RotCavtCrit for floating-point comparison
Called via mesh manipulation sub-callees (SetInputs, SetInputsForBEMT)

nwtc_types_used.note: Core mesh container for blade-node force/motion fields; used throughout AeroDyn blade-element I/O
Program descriptor (version/name) used in AD_Init output
Output-channel parameter type; used in AeroDyn output book-keeping
File metadata container; used during AeroDyn input parsing

integration_map.change: Declare BladeLoadCorrFactor as a ReKi scalar field in the AeroDyn ParameterType section so the Registry auto-generates it into AD_ParameterType in AeroDyn_Types.f90
Auto-generated by Registry after re-running the Registry tool; AD_ParameterType gains the BladeLoadCorrFactor field — no manual edits
In the primary-input-file reader, add a ReadVar call to parse BladeLoadCorrFactor from the AeroDyn input file and store it in InputFileData
In SetParameters, assign p%BladeLoadCorrFactor from InputFileData%BladeLoadCorrFactor to propagate from parsed input to the frozen parameter struct used at runtime
In RotCalcOutput, pass p%BladeLoadCorrFactor to BEMT_CalcOutput or apply the scalar correction to force/moment mesh nodes after BEMT_CalcOutput returns
In SetOutputsFromBEMT, multiply blade-element aerodynamic force components by BladeLoadCorrFactor before writing to the BladeLoad mesh — cleanest injection point, outside the BEMT solver loop
Lowest-level injection point: if factor is threaded into BEMT ParameterType, apply it inside BEMT_CalcOutput at the per-node force calculation loop
FAST_CalcOutput calls AD_CalcOutput here; no signature change needed — BladeLoadCorrFactor is internal to AD_ParameterType which is already passed through unchanged

In FAST_InitializeAll, AD_Init is invoked; no change required unless BladeLoadCorrFactor must be overridable from the FAST primary input, in which case add a pass-through in the AD InitInput struct assembly near the AD_Init call site
